channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: FBI investigates attacks in Michigan and Virginia. And, Senate passes housing bill
item.description: The FBI is investigating two separate attacks, one in Michigan and the other in Virginia, that happened yesterday. And, the Senate has passed the largest housing bill in decades.
item.content:encoded:
The FBI is investigating two separate attacks, one in Michigan and the other in Virginia, that happened yesterday. And, the Senate has passed the largest housing bill in decades.

(Image credit: Emily Elconin)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Countries are negotiating rules to mine the deep sea. The U.S. is pushing ahead alone
item.description: With growing interest in mining critical metals from the seafloor, countries are now negotiating international rules. The Trump administration is forging ahead on its own, speeding up environmental review for mining the fragile ecosystem.
item.content:encoded:
With growing interest in mining critical metals from the seafloor, countries are now negotiating international rules. The Trump administration is forging ahead on its own, speeding up environmental review for mining the fragile ecosystem.

(Image credit: NOAA Ocean Exploration)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Sommer

item.title: 4 confirmed dead after U.S. military aircraft goes down in Iraq
item.description: The U.S. Central Command confirmed that at least four of six crew members on the KC-135 aircraft were dead, after the refueling plane went down in western Iraq on Thursday.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Central Command confirmed that at least four of six crew members on the KC-135 aircraft were dead, after the refueling plane went down in western Iraq on Thursday.

(Image credit: Hussein Malla)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: It's Chalamet vs. ballet in this week's news quiz. Are your answers en pointe?
item.description: Meanwhile, if you've been paying attention to medicine, basketball and the British Parliament, you'll get at least three questions right this week.
item.content:encoded: Meanwhile, if you've been paying attention to medicine, basketball and the British Parliament, you'll get at least three questions right this week.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Trump wants more apprenticeships. An Arkansas manufacturer is giving it a try
item.description: President Trump has touted apprenticeships as part of his promise of a golden era for American workers. But are his administration's investments enough?
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has touted apprenticeships as part of his promise of a golden era for American workers. But are his administration's investments enough?

(Image credit: Joshua Danquah Asante for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Egg prices have taken a beating. What's behind the drop?
item.description: A year ago, eggs were scarce and prices were sky-high. But avian flu took a much smaller toll on America's egg-laying chickens this winter than last, and egg prices have tumbled 42%.
item.content:encoded:
A year ago, eggs were scarce and prices were sky-high. But avian flu took a much smaller toll on America's egg-laying chickens this winter than last, and egg prices have tumbled 42%.

(Image credit: American Egg Board)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: At the Winter Paralympics, some athletes have found business opportunities
item.description: At the Winter Paralympics, athletes with prosthetics often modify them to fit their bodies more precisely. That has led to some competitors starting their own businesses to help fellow amputees.
item.content:encoded:
At the Winter Paralympics, athletes with prosthetics often modify them to fit their bodies more precisely. That has led to some competitors starting their own businesses to help fellow amputees.

(Image credit: Emily Chen-Newton)
item.dc:creator: Emily Chen-Newton

item.title: Democrats set a turnout record in Texas, so is this the year it turns blue?
item.description: Latinos helped Texas Democrats set the new record for a primary, but the state has been a white whale for the party for decades.
item.content:encoded:
Latinos helped Texas Democrats set the new record for a primary, but the state has been a white whale for the party for decades.

(Image credit: Danielle Villasana)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Bucking stigma, more places turn to factory-built for affordable housing
item.description: Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.
item.content:encoded:
Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.

(Image credit: Anusha Mathur)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Medicaid can share data with ICE. Here's how that 180-degree change spreads fear
item.description: When Medicaid began sharing personal data with federal immigration authorities last year, it upended decades of explicit promises to patients. Now, even eligible immigrants fear getting the health coverage.
item.content:encoded:
When Medicaid began sharing personal data with federal immigration authorities last year, it upended decades of explicit promises to patients. Now, even eligible immigrants fear getting the health coverage.

(Image credit: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez)
item.dc:creator: Alex Olgin
